title: salvia lavandulifolia var. pyrenaeorum
intro: Salvia lavandulifolia var. pyrenaeorum is a lesser-known variety of the Salvia lavandulifolia plant, commonly known as Spanish sage, which is a perennial herb native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits associated with Salvia lavandulifolia var. pyrenaeorum include its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress, improve cognitive function, and protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
